title: The Top 10 Garbage Scorsese Movies and ‘The Alto Knights’
format: Chris Ryan joins Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins to bury 'The Alto Knights,' Barry Levinson's twin-De Niro mob movie, then build a top 10 of 'garbage Scorsese': the knockoffs that borrow Marty's style without his soul. Opens with a trailer-and-schedule news dump led by the new PTA teaser.

segments.heading: The news: trailers and a Warner Bros. schedule shuffle
segments.note: the PTA teaser, and everyone moving off August 8th

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's loose Vineland adaptation, moved off August 8th to September 26th (staying on the 2025 calendar), with a teaser dropping a day later. The hosts clocked Leonardo DiCaprio, a pregnant Teyonah Taylor firing a rifle, Sean Penn's voice, and Chase Infiniti; Sean says he's watched it 42 times. A likely fall-festival play, though the hosts note PTA has often held films back (Phantom Thread, Licorice Pizza) and wonder if Warner Bros. is gun-shy after the Joker sequel's Venice premiere.

films.title: Weapons
films.note: Zach Cregger's follow-up to Barbarian, described as a horror-movie version of Magnolia (a multi-character arc), moved up into the vacated August 8th slot.

films.title: Materialists
films.note: Celine Song's romance with Dakota Johnson, Chris Evans, and Pedro Pascal, out June 13. A matchmaker falls for two men; Broadcast News is cited as the signal inspiration.

films.title: F1
films.note: Joseph Kosinski's Brad Pitt racing movie. Sean is in the 'show me the movie' zone, wary of the rote framework and the 'I'm too old for this' beats, but trusting Kosinski's action.

films.title: Together
films.note: The Alison Brie / Dave Franco high-concept horror that was a Sundance sensation. Sean won't spoil past the logline.

films.title: The Phoenician Scheme
films.note: Wes Anderson's spy film, out in July. Michael Cera finally joins the troupe; Benicio del Toro and Scarlett Johansson are said to be aboard.

films.title: The Bride!
films.note: Maggie Gyllenhaal's musical take on the Bride of Frankenstein, pushed to 2026 after testing reportedly went badly.

films.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre
films.note: The 1974 original, back in the news: Strange Darling's J.T. Mollner and Glen Powell are said to be in pole position on a reboot, Powell producing and starring. Sean is dubious about Chainsaw as IP; Chris can't wait.

segments.heading: ‘The Alto Knights’
segments.note: Barry Levinson · De Niro times two · a movie that could have been an email

films.title: The Alto Knights
films.note: Barry Levinson's mob movie, written by Goodfellas scribe Nicholas Pileggi, with Robert De Niro in a dual role as 1950s bosses Vito Genovese and Frank Costello (a De Niro-driven choice that started life as a Costello solo piece). Also features Debra Messing, Cosmo Jarvis as Vinny 'The Chin' Gigante, Catherine Narducci, and Michael Rispoli as Albert Anastasia. Everyone was bored: Amanda unbelievably so, Chris despite loving the subject. Reads like Netflix true-crime: direct-to-camera exposition, archival photos, voiceover, covering ground Godfather II, Goodfellas, and The Irishman already owned. Credit to Dante Spinotti's warm photography and a near-photoreal makeup job. The hosts frame it as closing business at the end of a run: Levinson is 82, De Niro and Spinotti in their 80s, Pileggi in his 90s.

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: The source text hanging over everything: Pileggi wrote it, and Alto Knights fills the parts it skipped.

films.title: Casino
films.note: Costello is 'Ace Rothstein without Vegas,' per Sean, the diplomat operator.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: The signature old-man mafia movie and the melancholy comparison: The Irishman asked whether it was all worth it; Alto Knights just does the thing again.

films.title: Bugsy
films.note: Levinson's closest sibling film: Oscar-nominated, all the parts in place, and to Sean a juiceless slog, much like this one.

films.title: The Godfather Part II
films.note: Dramatized the Kefauver hearings first; Don Barzini and the Vito Corleone character draw on Genovese and Costello. 'We got it,' the hosts say.

films.title: Manhunter
films.note: Offered as proof of Spinotti's pedigree behind the camera.

films.title: Diner
films.note: The early Levinson mode, talky, character-first Baltimore dramedy, before a long mid-to-downfall stretch.

films.title: Rain Man
films.note: Levinson's Hollywood peak, another talky character piece.

segments.heading: The Top 10 Garbage Scorsese Movies
segments.note: loving knockoffs that take the style but not the soul

films.title: Magazine Dreams
films.note: Elijah Bynum's Jonathan Majors bodybuilder drama, finally released: 'what if Taxi Driver but a bodybuilder.' Sean's example that garbage Scorsese isn't only mob; it's the self-loathing, the doomed loner, the tonality.

films.title: The Bikeriders
films.note: The final 10, and the most recent pick. Jeff Nichols builds a Goodfellas-on-bikes out of a photo book: needle drops, detached narration, a man pulled between his domestic and criminal lives. Chris means the comparison as a compliment.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: The final 10. New York strippers running a scam, an after-the-fact interview framing device (Julia Stiles as journalist Jessica Pressler) standing in for narration, needle drops, some righteousness on their side.

films.title: Hot Summer Nights
films.note: The final 10. Elijah Bynum's Cape Cod drug-dealing coming-of-age with an early Timothée Chalamet: 'first-half Goodfellas.' Sean and Chris walked out of the South by Southwest premiere uneasy at how much of their taste was in it; Sean doesn't think it's good, but it's the platonic garbage Scorsese: snap zooms, tracking shots, bad narration, a rooting-interest dirtbag.

films.title: American Hustle
films.note: The final 10, 'emphasis on the garbagey.' David O. Russell doing '80s Scorsese energy: big cocaine, wall-to-wall voiceover stitching a fractured script. Amanda dislikes it but stands by Amy Adams.

films.title: American Gangster
films.note: The final 10, and the affectionate one. Ridley Scott's Frank Lucas movie (Denzel Washington and Russell Crowe circling each other) that everyone hoped would be Scott's Scorsese and turned out to be the garbage version. Josh Brolin's dirty cop is the highlight.

films.title: Alpha Dog
films.note: The final 10. Nick Cassavetes' true-crime kidnapping drama, an early Justin Timberlake, plus Ben Foster, Emile Hirsch, Bruce Willis, Amanda Seyfried. High energy that falls apart; Sean remembers being baffled by the hype.

films.title: Blow
films.note: The final 10. Ted Demme's Johnny Depp drug-trafficker saga: 'the most like Gimme Shelter needle-drop rip-off attempt.' Beloved by people who saw it at nine; not good, Sean insists.

films.title: Donnie Brasco
films.note: The final 10, a big one from the '90s boom. Mike Newell, the Brit from Four Weddings and a Funeral, directing Depp and Pacino undercover.

films.title: Amongst Friends
films.note: The final 10, and Sean's deep cut. Rob Weiss's Long Island crime movie about privileged kids drawn into a bookie grandfather's world: a baseball coach called it the best movie since Goodfellas. One mutual on Letterboxd (Dave Weigel) has logged it, at one star.

films.title: Laws of Gravity
films.note: The final 10, a Chris Ryan classic. Nick Gomez's $50,000 Brooklyn crime debut with a young Edie Falco and Peter Greene; Gomez later made New Jersey Drive and directed Homicide: Life on the Street.

films.title: Casino
films.note: The most honorable mention. Sean thinks it should have made the 10. Also the meta case: it's Scorsese doing garbage Scorsese, dismissed as 'this again?' on release.

films.title: You Were Never Really Here
films.note: Lynne Ramsay's Taxi Driver variation: 'what if the overhead slow-mo shot in Taxi Driver was an entire film.' Joaquin Phoenix as an avenging assassin; the Paul Schrader side of Scorsese rather than the frenetic one.

films.title: Observe and Report
films.note: Chris's where-does-Marty-end pick. Jody Hill's mall-cop movie fusing crime Scorsese with the deluded-clown King of Comedy Scorsese; Seth Rogen, plus a very funny Anna Faris.

films.title: Blood Ties
films.note: Guillaume Canet's 1974-set film, co-written by James Gray: 'the neighborhood over from Mean Streets,' with Clive Owen, Billy Crudup, Marion Cotillard, and a stacked '70s soundtrack (Lee Moses's 'Bad Girl').

films.title: Good Time
films.note: The Safdies. Chris throws it out not as garbage but as the good version, the kinetic energy of Mean Streets done right.

films.title: War Dogs
films.note: Todd Phillips' arms-dealer movie: all the pieces (a great Bradley Cooper), but the motivation is 'Miles Teller's baby needs a crib,' not Harvey Keitel's tortured soul. Style without theme.

films.title: The Good Shepherd
films.note: One of only two films De Niro directed (with A Bronx Tale). Eric Roth's entirely invented CIA history: the fictional man walking through every era of the agency.

films.title: A Bronx Tale
films.note: De Niro's directorial debut; qualifies as garbage Scorsese, though it's De Niro himself doing it.

films.title: Smokin' Aces
films.note: Joe Carnahan's overstuffed hitman ensemble: Ryan Reynolds, Andy Garcia, Alicia Keys as a sniper.

films.title: Running Scared
films.note: The Paul Walker version, with Vera Farmiga, a genuinely cool one on Sean's long list.

films.title: The Way of the Gun
films.note: Christopher McQuarrie's directorial debut after The Usual Suspects: Ryan Phillippe and Benicio del Toro, half Scorsese and half Tarantino.

referenced.group: Scorsese's own films, the measuring stick
'80s films in the lineage (good, not garbage)
